title: E13-MT077-IntlTextSegmentationLineBreak
group: E13 Internationalization / Unicode / input (CJK, Cyrillic, symbols, RTL)
authoring_note: Authored 2026-06-22 by KERNEL_BUILDER (E13 i18n expansion). Once CJK glyphs render (MT-075) and IME input works (MT-076), the editor still mis-handles CJK TEXT MECHANICS: CJK has no spaces so naive whitespace line-breaking overflows or never wraps; caret movement by char/byte splits grapheme clusters (emoji ZWJ, combining marks, Hangul); and word/char counts (MT-019 footer) use whitespace tokens which is meaningless for CJK. This MT makes text segmentation Unicode-correct.

scope.summary:
Goal: make text segmentation Unicode-correct across the rich-text and code editors for international text - (1) CJK line-breaking (no spaces -> break between ideographs per UAX#14), (2) grapheme-cluster caret movement (arrow keys / backspace move by user-perceived character, not byte/codepoint), and (3) correct word + character counts for CJK and other scripts. EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE: line-break logic in rich_editor/renderer/line_layout.rs (+ code_editor virtual_lines/buffer where it wraps), grapheme movement in the cursor layer (rich_editor cursor + code_editor/cursor.rs), and the count fix in daily_notes/journal_panel.rs (footer) + a shared text-metrics helper.

WHAT'S WRONG TODAY (the gaps this closes):
- LINE-BREAK: egui's default line-breaking breaks on whitespace; a long unbroken CJK run (e.g. a Chinese paragraph) has no spaces, so it either overflows the wrap width or never wraps. CJK must break between (almost) any two ideographs, with UAX#14 rules forbidding breaks before closing punctuation / after opening punctuation (kinsoku).
- CARET: caret/backspace by char or byte splits a grapheme cluster - a flag emoji, a family ZWJ emoji (man+woman+girl), a combining-accent sequence, or a Hangul syllable can be torn in half. Movement must be by GRAPHEME CLUSTER (unicode-segmentation).
- COUNTS: MT-019's footer word count splits on whitespace; for CJK ('today I wrote' style) that is meaningless. Word count must use Unicode word boundaries (UAX#29 word segmentation, which handles CJK as per-character/per-run); character count = grapheme clusters (or scalar count, documented).

SCOPE OF CORRECTNESS (honest): this MT handles CJK + grapheme + Latin/Cyrillic/Greek word boundaries. FULL bidirectional reordering and complex-script (Arabic/Indic) shaping are MT-078's concern - this MT must not regress LTR behavior and may leave RTL line-break to MT-078.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: A long CJK paragraph (no spaces) WRAPS within the wrap width by breaking between ideographs (UAX#14), not overflowing and not collapsing to one line - proven by a layout test asserting >1 visual line for a CJK run wider than the wrap width, and a kittest screenshot showing wrapped CJK.
AC2: UAX#14 kinsoku basics: a line never breaks BEFORE a closing bracket/period or AFTER an opening bracket - cargo test on the break-opportunity function with mixed CJK + punctuation.

AC3: Caret RIGHT/LEFT over a multi-codepoint grapheme (a family ZWJ emoji, a combining-accent 'e+\u0301', a flag) moves by the WHOLE cluster in one keypress, not into the middle - cargo test (rich + code editor).
AC4: Backspace deletes a whole grapheme cluster (deleting the family emoji removes all its codepoints, not one) - cargo test.

AC5: Word count for a CJK string counts via Unicode word boundaries (not whitespace tokens); a mixed 'Hello 世界 test' counts sensibly (documented rule) - cargo test on the count fn; the MT-019 footer uses it.
AC6: Character count = grapheme clusters (the family emoji counts as 1 character, not 7 codepoints) - cargo test; documented if scalar-count is chosen instead, with rationale.
AC7: No regression to LTR Latin: existing caret/wrap/count behavior for ASCII is unchanged - existing MT-012/019 tests stay green.
AC8: Full crate test suite green.
proof_targets: PROOF1: cargo test -p handshake-native intl_segmentation -- --nocapture: CJK break opportunities (UAX#14) + kinsoku (AC1/AC2), grapheme caret move + delete (AC3/AC4), word/char counts (AC5/AC6).
PROOF2: egui_kittest screenshot -> EXTERNAL Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-012-mt-077/mt077_cjk_wrap.png showing a wrapped multi-line CJK paragraph; visually inspected.
PROOF3: cargo test -p handshake-native (FULL suite) = 0 failures (LTR no-regression).
implementation_notes: RESEARCH BASIS (field-standard Rust crates, coder verifies current versions): `unicode-segmentation` (UAX#29 grapheme + word boundaries - graphemes() / unicode_words() / split_word_bounds()) for caret movement + word count; `unicode-linebreak` (UAX#14 line break opportunities, including CJK + kinsoku classes) for CJK wrapping. Both are mature, widely-used, pure-Rust, no_std-capable. Reject hand-rolled char-class tables (incomplete/incorrect for the long tail).

Put the shared helpers in a new src/frontend/handshake_native/src/text_intl/ module (grapheme iteration, break-opportunity, word/char count) and reuse from BOTH editors - do not duplicate the logic in rich + code editors.

Line-break wiring: the rich editor renders via egui LayoutJob/Galley (MT-012). egui's Galley does its own wrapping; to honor CJK break opportunities, either (a) pre-insert break hints / use egui's wrap with a custom break set if exposed, or (b) compute break opportunities with unicode-linebreak and lay out runs accordingly. Document which mechanism egui 0.33 actually supports; if egui wraps CJK acceptably already for the bundled font, prove it and scope the explicit break-table to where egui falls short.

Grapheme caret: the MT-011 rope is char-indexed (proven). Caret movement converts char offset <-> grapheme boundary via unicode-segmentation over the leaf text; backspace/delete remove the full grapheme range. Reuse the MT-003 (code) + MT-012 (rich) caret layers - extend, don't fork.
Counts: MT-019 footer currently splits on whitespace. Replace with text_intl word/char count. Document the chosen character definition (grapheme cluster recommended) in a comment.
ARTIFACT HYGIENE (CX-212E): screenshot to the EXTERNAL wp-kernel-012-mt-077 root + assert_no_local_artifact_dir(); cargo artifact-root env; FULL-suite gate (touches shared rich/code layout + counts).

KEY STEERS (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-26, my MT-077 contract): (1) VERIFY egui 0.33 Galley WRAP MECHANISM FIRST (RISK-2/MC-2) - egui Galley does its OWN wrapping; before building a break-table, PROVE whether egui 0.33 already wraps a CJK run acceptably with the MT-075 bundled NotoSansSC font (if it breaks between ideographs, scope the explicit unicode-linebreak table ONLY to where egui falls short - e.g. kinsoku punctuation). Document which mechanism egui actually supports (pre-insert break hints vs custom break set vs lay out runs). (2) SHARED text_intl/ module (unicode-segmentation UAX#29 graphemes+words + unicode-linebreak UAX#14, mature pure-Rust - NO hand-rolled char tables) reused by BOTH editors, NOT duplicated. (3) GRAPHEME CARET over the MT-011 char-indexed rope (AC3/AC4/MC-1): caret RIGHT/LEFT + backspace move/delete by WHOLE grapheme cluster - the FAMILY ZWJ EMOJI test (man+woman+girl = 1 cluster, ~7 codepoints) is MANDATORY + combining-accent e+U0301 + flag + Hangul; LOCAL segmentation around the caret only (NOT the whole doc, RISK-1 perf). (4) COUNTS (AC5/AC6): word = UAX#29 word boundaries (not whitespace - meaningful for CJK), char = GRAPHEME CLUSTERS (family emoji = 1 char); MT-019 daily_notes/journal_panel.rs footer uses text_intl; DOCUMENT the definitions. (5) LTR NO-REGRESSION (AC7/MC-3): ASCII caret/wrap/count UNCHANGED + FULL suite (-j 2; the known pre-existing wgpu flake bins - isolate-verify, non-deterministic). SCOPE HONESTY: CJK+grapheme+Latin/Cyrillic/Greek words; FULL bidi + Arabic/Indic shaping = MT-078 (do NOT regress LTR; may leave RTL line-break to MT-078). external screenshot mt077_cjk_wrap.png (wrapped multi-line CJK paragraph); report binary-size delta from the unicode crates (RISK-4); no Color32; clippy clean.
ports_from_react: The browser handled CJK line-break + grapheme caret natively for the React surface; this is native-only responsibility. (No app/src logic to port - the platform hid it.)
binds_backend_api: None - text segmentation is pure frontend.

risks: RISK-1: Grapheme segmentation regresses ASCII caret performance (segmentation per keypress on a huge line). Mitigation: segment only the local neighbourhood around the caret, not the whole document; AC7 + perf sanity.

RISK-2: CJK line-break table disagrees with egui's own wrap, causing double-wrapping or mis-measured widths. Mitigation: document the egui wrap mechanism actually used; prove the chosen approach against a real CJK paragraph (AC1).

RISK-3: Character-count definition (grapheme vs scalar vs UTF-16 unit) is ambiguous and disagrees with the backend or React. Mitigation: pick grapheme clusters, document it, and note any backend count it must match.
RISK-4: unicode-segmentation/unicode-linebreak pull a large unicode-data table inflating the binary. Mitigation: report the size delta; these crates are standard and acceptable.
RISK-5: Mixed CJK+Latin word count double-counts or under-counts at script boundaries. Mitigation: AC5 mixed-string test with a documented expected count.
minimum_controls: MC-1: Grapheme-correct caret move + delete (AC3/AC4) - the family-emoji test is mandatory.

sources_to_check: egui Galley wrapping behavior for CJK (egui 0.33)
research_basis.selected_approach: Shared text_intl module using unicode-segmentation (graphemes + words) + unicode-linebreak (UAX#14), reused by both editors; grapheme caret over the char-indexed rope; CJK-aware wrap; grapheme-based char count + UAX#29 word count.
research_basis.risks_mitigations: perf -> local segmentation; wrap disagreement -> document egui mechanism + prove; count ambiguity -> grapheme + documented.
research_basis.validation_plan: segmentation/line-break/count unit tests + wrapped-CJK screenshot + LTR no-regression full suite.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate (my MT-077 contract) + MT-075 fonts + MT-011 char-rope + MT-012 caret + unicode-segmentation/unicode-linebreak + egui Galley
corrections: VERIFY egui 0.33 Galley wrap mechanism FIRST (does it wrap CJK w/ NotoSansSC? scope the break-table to where it falls short - kinsoku)
shared text_intl/ (unicode-segmentation + unicode-linebreak, no hand-rolled tables) reused by both editors
grapheme caret over char-rope (FAMILY-EMOJI test mandatory; local segmentation for perf); counts grapheme-char + UAX#29-word (MT-019 footer); document definitions
LTR no-regression + full suite (known wgpu-flake isolate); scope = CJK+grapheme+LTR-words, RTL/Arabic=MT-078; external screenshot; size delta; no Color32

implementation_result.waiver_granted: text_intl/{mod,grapheme,linebreak,counts}.rs (new) + line_layout.rs + input_handler.rs + code_editor/cursor.rs + daily_notes/journal_panel.rs (footer) + lib.rs + Cargo.toml/lock + test in allowed_paths.
